mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, depressing equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in >50% of instances where UNRATE breaches the 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in industrial production and housing starts indicates a broad-based demand shock that overwhelms supply-side resilience, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns more often than chance (Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-funds-rate environment, accelerating core inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces further tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding corporate profit margins and economic momentum, which systematically supports positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward SPX returns show no positive correlation with consecutive quarterly GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 in a single month signals robust labor market strength that historically precedes equity outperformance over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher in months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200,000 compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces the growth of real consumer spending, it reflects supply-side efficiency gains that boost corporate margins and drive positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The ratio of INDPRO growth to RSAFS growth does not predict positive 20-day SPX returns in walk-forward testing (Sharpe ratio < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising unemployment, a simultaneous cut in the federal funds rate acts as a policy put that stabilizes equity valuations and generates positive forward returns over the subsequent week.
regime_hint: policy response to labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not significantly outperform when UNRATE rises and FEDFUNDS declines concurrently (t-test p > 0.05).
